topic.what: Integral cryptanalysis and key recovery on reduced-round Kuznyechik without pre-whitening
topic.how: Uses first-order 28-structure to derive 2-round balanced property, extends to 3-round via one-round inversion, converts to 4-round key-recovery using byte-wise balanced tests with L inverse and S inverse
topic.why_matters: Assesses security of Russian standardized cipher under modern cryptanalytic techniques; provides first practical 4-round key-recovery without pre-whitening

characteristics.vs_baseline: Lower than Biryukov et al. 7-round (2^128) and ElSheikh 4-round (2^120)

characteristics.evidence: 16 × 28 × 28 per structure; dominates as 2^20 inverse S-box ops
characteristics.context: 4-round key recovery; extension to 5 rounds requires 2^128

characteristics.vs_baseline: Deterministic for correct key; false positives 2^-8 per byte after two structures
characteristics.evidence: Two structures reduce expected candidates to unique value with high probability
characteristics.context: Algorithm 4 verified with example master key

characteristics.vs_baseline: Extends 3-round distinguisher to key recovery; vs 7-round Biryukov (data-intensive)
characteristics.evidence: Algorithm 4 and experimental validation in Sage/Python
characteristics.context: Without pre-whitening; extension to 5 rounds infeasible (2^128 lower bound)

good_for: understanding pre-whitening impact on integral attacks
byte-wise key recovery via balanced tests
not_for: extending beyond 4 rounds with this approach
requires: access to reduced-round encryption oracle without pre-whitening
ability to generate structured plaintext sets

novelty: first-order structure extension to 3-round bytewise-balanced distinguisher via one-round inversion
conversion of 3-round balanced property to practical 4-round key-recovery
byte-wise independent key recovery using balanced XOR condition after L inverse
analysis of why extension beyond 4 rounds yields 2^128 complexity
